note: Container api started — EVIL_REGEX_ACTIVE=0, service healthy
Baseline healthcheck pass — p99 < 10ms, CPU normal
WAF rule deployment initiated — EVIL_REGEX_ACTIVE flipped to 1, container force-recreated
New container starts with evil regex middleware active — every request now triggers backtracking
First request with adversarial query string hits WAF middleware — regex engine enters catastrophic backtracking, p99 spikes from 8ms to 8200ms
Prometheus p99 latency crosses 500ms threshold (actual ~8200ms) — alert fires after 1-minute rate window
Container CPU pegged at 100% — regex engine consuming all available CPU cycles on backtracking
Container CPU throttle metric crosses 80% for sustained window — secondary confirmation of CPU saturation
RCA output: api service degraded, root cause identified as CPU exhaustion at middleware layer — NOT a downstream service failure
HTTP connection queue growing — incoming requests pile behind blocked regex processing, uvicorn event loop starved
Operator sets EVIL_REGEX_ACTIVE=0 and force-recreates container to simulate rollback mitigation
Mitigated container restarts — WAF middleware disabled, service restored
p99 latency drops back to 8ms — full recovery confirmed within 5s of rollback
Pipeline confirms recovery — healthz responds < 10ms, CPU usage nominal
